zh.Haverhill, Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Co: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼諾福爾可哈維爾爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Heath於1603年出生在英國艾可納時，他的父親Sir Henry John Heath29歲，母親Alis Bartholomew28歲。他於1619年與Dorithie Ryle在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1675年1月17日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼諾福爾可哈維爾爾享年72歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Heath于1603年出生在英国艾可納时，他的父亲Sir Henry John Heath29岁，母亲Alis Bartholomew28岁。他于1619年与Dorithie Ryle在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1675年1月17日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼諾福爾可哈維爾爾享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: Heath
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) heath’ (Middle English he(a)th, Old English hǣth) or else a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. The same word also denoted heather, the characteristic plant of heathland areas. This surname has also been established in Dublin, Ireland, since the late 16th century.

variants.name: Haith
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): variant of   Heath  .

variants.name: Heathman
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a dweller ‘on the heath’, from Middle English he(a)th + man(ne). Compare Heath and Heather .

variants.name: Hatton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Hatton (Cheshire, Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Shropshire, Staffordshire, Warwickshire), Hatton Hall (Cheshire), or Cold and High Hatton (Shropshire). The placenames derive from Old English hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Heath .
English: variant of Atton with prosthetic H-, a topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)). Compare Towne .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Chatáin, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of (Saint) Catan’; see McHatton .

variants.name: Heather
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller on the heath’, an agent derivative of Middle English hethe; compare Heath . The name may also be habitational name from Heather (Leicestershire), from Old English hǣthor ‘heathland’ (hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + the noun suffix -or) or hǣddre ‘heather’. This surname is widespread in southern England, and also established in Ireland.

variants.name: Health
meanings: Altered form of English and Scottish   Heath  .
English and Scottish: perhaps a nickname from the vocabulary word health. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is 105.

variants.name: Hoad
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, from Middle English hoth ‘heath’ (Old English hāth, a byform of Old English hǣth), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Hoath in Kent. This form was restricted in the Middle Ages to southeastern England, and the surname is still largely confined to Kent, Surrey, and Sussex. Compare Heath .

variants.name: Heid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Eckhart, Gerd, Gotthilf, Heinz, Klaus, Konrad, Kurt, Manfred, Ralf, Uli.
German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name from Middle High German heide, German Heide ‘heath, moor, open, uncultivated land’. Compare Heath .
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Haido, a short form of any of various compound names beginning with haid ‘heath’ (i.e. wasteland, often with heather).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When John Heath was born in 1603, in Nazeing, Essex, England, his father, Sir Henry John Heath, was 29 and his mother, Alis Bartholomew, was 28. He married Dorithie Ryle in 1619, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 17 January 1675, in Haverhill, Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 72.

ko.Haverhill, Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Co: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 노르폴크 하버힐
ko.lifeSummary: John Heath(이)가 1603년 영국 에세스 나즈인그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Henry John Heath(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Alis Bartholomew(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1619년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Dorithie Ryle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1675년 1월 17일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 노르폴크 하버힐에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Haverhill, Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Co: хавирилл, норфолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Heath родился в 1603 г. в местности назинг, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Henry John Heath было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Alis Bartholomew, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Dorithie Ryle в 1619 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 17 января 1675 г. в местности хавирилл, норфолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 72 лет.
